contextBlurb: Newfoundland and Labrador's housing policy is anchored by the Newfoundland and Labrador Housing Corporation (NLHC), which administers programs addressing homelessness, transitional housing, and low-income rental support. The provincial government introduced a Special Debate on Housing in 2026, responding to a 71 per cent growth in the social housing waitlist between 2021 and 2025. Emergency rental assistance of up to $1,500 per household per month has also been made available through provincial releases.

legislation.title: Federal-Provincial Housing and Homelessness Project
legislation.summary: A federal-provincial housing project announced by NLHC as part of ongoing efforts to address housing and homelessness challenges across the province.

legislation.title: Home Modification Program
legislation.summary: Provides funding to assist homeowners with low-to-moderate income to modify their homes, administered through the Department of Social Supports and Well-Being.

legislation.title: Rental Housing Program for Low-Income Households
legislation.summary: A provincial program providing rental housing support for low-income households, listed under the Government of Newfoundland and Labrador's housing programs and services.

legislation.title: Newfoundland and Labrador Disability Benefit
legislation.summary: Provides up to $400 per month ($4,800 annually) beginning July 2025 to qualifying individuals, contributing to housing affordability for persons with disabilities.

legislation.title: Federal-Provincial Housing and Homelessness Initiative
legislation.summary: A joint federal-provincial housing project administered through the NLHC to address housing and homelessness challenges in Newfoundland and Labrador.

legislation.title: Home Modification Program
legislation.summary: Provides funding to assist homeowners with low-to-moderate incomes to modify their homes, administered through the Newfoundland and Labrador Housing Corporation.

legislation.title: Rental Housing Program for Low-Income Households
legislation.summary: A provincial program providing subsidized rental housing options for low-income individuals and families in Newfoundland and Labrador.

legislation.title: Emergency Rental Assistance Program
legislation.summary: Provides up to $1,500 per household per month to assist with the cost of rental accommodations, with individuals required to provide receipts to confirm rental expenses.

legislation.title: Rental Accommodation Assistance Program
legislation.summary: Provides up to $1,500 per household per month to assist eligible individuals with the cost of rental accommodations, with receipts required to confirm rental costs.

legislation.title: Special Debate on Housing – Waitlist and Supply Initiative
legislation.summary: A provincial government special debate on housing introduced to address the social housing waitlist, which grew 71 per cent between 2021 and 2025, focusing on increasing housing supply and reviewing waitlist criteria.

legislation.title: Special Debate on Housing
legislation.summary: The provincial government introduced a special legislative debate on housing, focusing on addressing the social housing waitlist that grew 71 per cent between 2021 and 2025 and increasing housing supply.

legislation.title: Transitional and Supportive Living Program RFP – St. John's
legislation.summary: A Request for Proposals issued by the NLHC for the creation of a new Transitional and Supportive Living Program in St. John's to help those experiencing homelessness achieve housing stability.

legislation.title: Request for Proposals: Transitional and Supportive Living Program in St. John's
legislation.summary: A formal RFP issued by the NLHC to create a new Transitional and Supportive Living Program in St. John's, aimed at helping individuals experiencing homelessness achieve housing stability.
